definition: Central Bank Dealers Range — the price range formed between 2:00 PM and 8:00 PM New York time. This is when institutional algorithms reset and set the next day's reference range.
depends_on: sessions_kill_zones (CBDR occurs between NY close and Asian open)
feeds_into: daily_bias (CBDR range direction hints at next day bias)
venom_model (Venom uses pre-market range which extends CBDR concept)
related_to: PO3_AMD (CBDR IS pre-accumulation — the reset before Asian accumulation)
asian_range (Asian range typically contains or extends CBDR)

projections.definition: Standard deviation projections above and below the CBDR range provide probable target zones for the next session.
projections.1_SD: 68% probability price reaches this level — primary target zone
projections.2_SD: 95% probability boundary — strong reversal zone if reached

how_to_use: Map the CBDR range, then project SDs. If price reaches 2 SD during London/NY, expect mean reversion. Use SD projections as TP targets or reversal zones.
